touches.ai_summary: Cold outbound email sent to Amanda Young at PixelHealth introducing our platform's pipeline visibility capabilities. No response received.
Follow-up cold email to Amanda highlighting differentiation from Gong with specific pipeline automation features. No response received.
Third outbound email with case study from similar SaaS company. Amanda responded expressing interest in learning more about pipeline visibility tools.

questions_asked.question: What makes your approach different from Gong?
questions_asked.answer: Focused on explaining our unlimited automation tier and real-time pipeline insights vs. post-call analytics focus

next_steps: Amanda agreed to schedule qualification call

touches.ai_summary: Pre-call email sent confirming qualification call agenda. Amanda confirmed attendance and shared she's been in VP of Sales role for 3 months, looking to improve forecast accuracy.

questions_asked.question: What are your current pipeline visibility challenges?
questions_asked.answer: Amanda mentioned inconsistent deal stage progression and difficulty forecasting with current tools

touches.ai_summary: 12-minute qualification call with Amanda. Discussed current state with basic CRM, need for better pipeline visibility as she ramps in new role. Team of 11 sales reps struggling with forecast accuracy. Budget concerns mentioned but not blocking.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this?
questions_asked.answer: Amanda mentioned Hazel Clark in implementation/procurement would need to be looped in later

questions_asked.question: How quickly can we get this implemented?

touches.ai_summary: Discovery follow-up email with ROI calculator and pricing for 11 seats. Amanda responded positively, wants to move to technical evaluation to see the platform in action.

questions_asked.question: Can we do a trial with a few reps before full rollout?
questions_asked.answer: Explained our evaluation process includes hands-on technical eval sessions

touches.ai_summary: 44-minute technical evaluation with Amanda. Walked through pipeline visibility dashboard, automation rules, and Gong integration comparison. Amanda engaged positively with unlimited automation tier, asked detailed questions about data sources and reporting.

questions_asked.question: How does this integrate with our existing Salesforce instance?
questions_asked.answer: Demonstrated native Salesforce connector and real-time sync

questions_asked.question: What's included in unlimited automation vs. your other tiers?
questions_asked.answer: Walked through tier comparison, highlighted workflow customization

questions_asked.question: What would success look like in the first 90 days?
questions_asked.answer: Amanda focused on forecast accuracy improvement and rep adoption metrics

objections: Expressed concern about change management with team already learning new processes under her leadership
next_steps: Schedule follow-up eval to show advanced features

touches.ai_summary: 48-minute deep-dive technical evaluation covering advanced automation workflows and custom reporting. Amanda brought specific use cases from her team. Discussion touched on pricing, she mentioned budget is tight but sees value.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize the pipeline stage definitions?
What does onboarding support look like?
questions_asked.answer: Outlined dedicated implementation support and training sessions

questions_asked.question: Should we get Hazel involved at this stage?
questions_asked.answer: Amanda said she wants to present the business case first, then bring in procurement

objections: Mentioned Gong is offering aggressive pricing to keep their business
next_steps: Amanda to build internal business case

touches.ai_summary: 20-minute executive meeting to review proposal. Only Amanda attended - no other executives joined. Discussed $8,900 ACV proposal with 29.9% discount to match competitive pressure. Amanda appreciative of pricing flexibility but seemed cautious about moving forward without broader buy-in.

questions_asked.question: Can we schedule time with your leadership team to align on strategic value?
questions_asked.answer: Amanda said she'd prefer to champion this internally first given she's still building credibility in the role

questions_asked.question: Is this the best you can do on pricing?
questions_asked.answer: Confirmed the 29.9% discount is our maximum flexibility

next_steps: Amanda to review proposal with Hazel
Target decision by end of week

touches.ai_summary: 29-minute negotiation call with Amanda. She's pushing for additional discount beyond the 29.9% already offered, citing Gong's competing proposal. Discussed contract terms and implementation timeline. Still no executive sponsor engaged in the process.

questions_asked.question: Can you include an additional 10% discount for annual prepayment?
questions_asked.answer: Explained we're already at maximum discount threshold

questions_asked.question: What happens if we want to add seats mid-year?
questions_asked.answer: Outlined prorated pricing structure for seat expansion

questions_asked.question: When can we get final decision-makers involved?
questions_asked.answer: Amanda said she has approval authority for this deal size but seemed hesitant

objections: Gong is offering more aggressive terms and Amanda's CFO is questioning the ROI
next_steps: Amanda to finalize with Hazel and procurement

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with updated contract and implementation timeline. Amanda responded asking for a few more days to finalize internal approvals. Mentioned Hazel has some procurement questions about payment terms.

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly payments instead of annual?
questions_asked.answer: Confirmed we can accommodate quarterly billing with standard terms

next_steps: Amanda and Hazel to review final contract
